title: FM-20 observer baseline staleness cascade — audit and automated detection
method: Systematic scan of all tools/*.py for hardcoded session-number patterns (fallback constants, epoch markers, estimated_sessions). Cross-reference each against current session to compute staleness. Build automated scanner wired into orient.py for continuous monitoring.
expect: FM-20 baseline staleness >80 sessions mean; build automated refresh reducing to <50

critical_time_bombs.pattern: hardcoded fallback at module level — affects all anxiety-zone calculations when git detection fails
critical_time_bombs.fix: APPLIED — replaced with 5-commit scan + 0 fallback + stderr warning

critical_time_bombs.pattern: estimated_sessions fallback — used when INDEX.md read fails

critical_time_bombs.fix: NOT APPLIED — only used when JSON file missing (acceptable)

critical_time_bombs.pattern: sink_count from S335 — hardcoded graph metric
critical_time_bombs.fix: NOT APPLIED — flagged by new scanner for future fix
NOT NEEDED — fixed historical marker, not a drifting baseline

hardening_actions: frontier_triage.py: replaced hardcoded S393 fallback with 5-commit git scan + safe 0 fallback + stderr warning
eval_sufficiency.py: replaced hardcoded 193 estimated_sessions with safe 1 default
orient_checks.py: added check_stale_baselines() scanner function — detects session_constant, fallback_comment, estimated_sessions, session_default patterns
orient.py: wired stale_baselines check into orient output

details: scaling_model.py:203 = 158 (269s stale, needs dynamic graph metric)
post_hardening.automated_detection: orient.py now flags any tool with hardcoded session >50 sessions behind current

actual: Mean staleness 173 sessions (exceeds >80 expect). 2 CRITICAL/HIGH fixes applied. Automated scanner wired. 5→2 stale baselines (1 acceptable epoch, 1 needs dynamic metric).
diff: Mean staleness exceeded expect (173 > 80). Surprise: lane_history.py ENFORCEMENT_SESSION is a valid historical epoch, not a drifting baseline — false positive class. Pattern: fallback constants and estimated_sessions are the real time-bombs; epoch constants are benign.

swiss_cheese_layers.after: 1 automated layer (orient.py stale-baselines scanner). FM-20: UNMITIGATED → MINIMAL
